stories.headline: Israel approves death penalty for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks
stories.summary: Israel's parliament passed a law making death by hanging the default sentence for Palestinians convicted in military courts of deadly attacks against Israelis. The law applies to West Bank Palestinians found guilty of intentionally carrying out deadly attacks deemed "acts of terrorism" by an Israeli military court. The bill's passage marks the culmination of a years-long push by Israel's far-right to escalate punishment for Palestinians convicted of nationalistic offenses. Human rights groups and Palestinian leaders have condemned the measure, calling it harsh. The law fulfills a pledge by Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, with far-right politicians long seeking its implementation. The move has been met with widespread criticism, with many viewing it as an escalation of tensions in the region. The law's passage has significant implications for the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

stories.headline: Spain has closed its airspace to US planes amid the Iran war?
stories.summary: Spain has closed its airspace to US planes involved in attacks on Iran, a step beyond its previous denial of use of jointly-operated military bases. The move is the latest in the country's opposition to the US-Israel conflict with Iran. Earlier, Spain had denied the US use of its jointly-operated military bases for the conflict. The closure of airspace to US military aircraft has caused tension with NATO ally, the US. Spain's decision is seen as a significant step in its opposition to the conflict, and it has drawn attention to the country's stance on the issue. The move affects US planes involved in the Iran war, and it is part of Spain's efforts to distance itself from the conflict.

stories.headline: They lied, they're dead: White House mocks slain Iran leaders over failed talks
stories.summary: The White House has made explosive remarks linking the deaths of Iranian leaders to failed negotiations, despite ongoing backchannel talks. The comments suggest that Iranian leaders were killed due to misleading the US in negotiations. Meanwhile, the US has threatened consequences if Iran fails to honor its commitments. The White House claims that talks with Iran are "going well" despite Iranian rhetoric denying direct negotiations. Tensions between the two countries continue to escalate, with the US issuing military warnings. Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has been involved in meetings in Tehran, amid the rising tensions. The situation remains volatile, with the White House touting progress in Iran talks while Tehran denies any direct negotiations are taking place. Backchannel diplomacy continues, but the direction of the talks remains uncertain.

stories.headline: Iran Update Special Report, March 30, 2026 US President Donald Trump told the Financial Times on March 30 that the combined force has struck 13,000 targets since the start of the war
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump stated that the combined force has struck 13,000 targets in Iran since the start of the war on February 28. The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) reported striking 170 targets over the past day. The combined force has continued to target senior Iranian officials, including the head of research at the Organization of Defensive Innovation and Research (SPND), Ali Fuladvand, who was killed in airstrikes on March 28. President Trump claimed progress in talks to end the war but warned of intense attacks on Iranian infrastructure targets if a deal is not reached. Trump also suggested considering a move to seize Iran's oil, contributing to volatility in oil and stock markets. The conflict continues to escalate, with ongoing airstrikes and tensions between the US, Israel, and Iran.

stories.headline: iPhone 18 Pro leak suggests smaller dynamic island cutout Leaked iPhone 18 Pro screen protector images hint at a narrower Dynamic Island.
stories.summary: Leaked images of an iPhone 18 Pro screen protector suggest a smaller Dynamic Island cutout. The cutout, which houses the TrueDepth camera and Face ID components, appears significantly slimmer than the current generation. Reports indicate the new cutout could be around 13.49mm, compared to approximately 20.76mm on the current model. This potential design change has sparked debate around Apple's choices. The iPhone 18 Pro is also rumored to have less color difference on the back glass. The leaked images and reports have been emerging over the past day, with some conflicting information about the Dynamic Island's size. However, the latest images of the screen protector seem to confirm a narrower cutout, sparking fresh discussion about the upcoming iPhone's design. The changes are expected to be part of the iPhone 18 Pro's major updates next year.

stories.headline: Android's new developer verification rollout begins, sideloading changes are next Google has started rolling out Android's new developer verification, with user-facing sideloading changes arriving later this year.
stories.summary: Google has begun rolling out Android's new developer verification process, allowing developers to register themselves and their apps. This verification is required for apps to be installed and updated on certified Android devices in certain regions, including Brazil, Indonesia, Singapore, and Thailand, starting September 30, 2026. Unregistered apps can still be sideloaded using ADB or an advanced flow. The verification process will roll out in phases, starting in select regions, with global expansion planned for 2027. Later this year, Android will introduce user-facing sideloading changes, requiring users to go through an advanced flow to install unverified apps. Developers can now verify themselves and their apps to ensure safe sideloading for users. The rollout aims to enhance security and safety for Android users by verifying the authenticity of apps and their developers.

stories.headline: Children’s screen use surged significantly during and after pandemic First systematic review to track long-term trends across pre- and post-pandemic periods finds dramatic rise in screen use among children and adolescents.
stories.summary: A systematic review conducted at the University of Turku, Finland, found a significant increase in screen use among children and adolescents during and after the COVID-19 pandemic. The review, published in Clinical Child Psychology and Psychiatry, analyzed long-term trends in screen time use among children and adolescents, including pre- and post-pandemic periods. Results showed a dramatic rise in screen use, with a clear increase occurring after the pandemic onset. The review also examined the association between screen time and sleep quality, as well as attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) prevalence. Studies included in the review compared screen time between children and adolescents with ADHD and those with typical development, with meta-analyses considering screen time of two hours or more per day. The findings highlight the need for awareness about the potential negative effects of excessive screen time on children's health.

stories.headline: Google Drive Brings AI Ransomware Detection to Stop File Attacks Before They Spread Google Drive now uses AI ransomware detection to pause syncing, alert users, and restore clean file versions to prevent and recover from attacks.
stories.summary: Google Drive has introduced an AI-powered ransomware detection feature to prevent and recover from file attacks. The capability uses artificial intelligence to identify encryption patterns associated with ransomware attacks, significantly improving detection accuracy and speed. When ransomware is detected, Google Drive pauses syncing, alerts users, and restores clean file versions to prevent the attack from spreading. The feature provides real-time detection, automatically pausing syncing before encrypted files reach the cloud, and allows users to quickly recover clean versions of files without losing important data. This AI-based protection is now generally available in Google Drive for desktop, helping to protect users from ransomware attacks and minimize data loss. The feature is designed to provide fast and effective protection against ransomware threats.

stories.headline: Wembanyama sets record as Spurs beat Bulls, Shai rescues Thunder Victor Wembanyama bagged a season-high 41 points and scored the fastest double-double in NBA history as the San Antonio Spurs rolled to a ninth straight victory
stories.summary: Victor Wembanyama scored a season-high 41 points and achieved the fastest double-double in NBA history, leading the San Antonio Spurs to a 129-114 win over the Chicago Bulls. The Spurs' ninth straight victory was fueled by a dominant second quarter, where they outscored the Bulls 35-19. This decisive performance allowed the Spurs to pull away and secure the win. Wembanyama's record-breaking double-double and 41-point performance were key factors in the team's success. The win marked the Spurs' ninth consecutive victory, with their strong offense proving too much for the Bulls to handle. The final score was 129-114 in favor of the Spurs, with Wembanyama's impressive performance being a major highlight of the game.

stories.headline: Warriors Star Stephen Curry Dragged Into Jaden Ivey Controversy Warriors star Stephen Curry was unexpectedly pulled into Jaden Ivey’s comments after the Bulls waived the former guard.
stories.summary: Warriors star Stephen Curry was unexpectedly pulled into a controversy surrounding Jaden Ivey after the Chicago Bulls waived the former guard. Ivey made comments about Curry's faith on Instagram Live, sparking an ugly dispute. The situation has drawn attention to Curry, who is currently dealing with his own return to the court, with his status for the play-in game still unclear. The Warriors are considering sitting Curry if he doesn't recover soon. Meanwhile, the team is reportedly interested in bringing back head coach Steve Kerr for another season, with Curry expressing his desire to reunite with his coach. The controversy surrounding Ivey's comments has added to the current uncertainty surrounding the Warriors and Curry's situation. The team's plans for the play-in game and next season remain uncertain.

stories.headline: Wales v Northern Ireland: The match nobody wants Wales host Northern Ireland in a friendly in Cardiff on Tuesday because both teams lost their World Cup play-off semi-finals on Thursday.
stories.summary: Wales hosts Northern Ireland in a friendly match at Cardiff City Stadium on Tuesday at 7:45 pm. The match was scheduled after both teams lost their World Cup play-off semi-finals on Thursday. The friendly is seen as a consolation match, as both teams had hoped to be competing for a World Cup spot. The match will be broadcast on BBC television and radio, with live text updates and score tracking available. Wales and Northern Ireland will face off in what is expected to be a subdued atmosphere, given their recent World Cup play-off losses. The Cardiff City Stadium will host the match, which kicks off at 7:45 pm on Tuesday, March 31. The teams will look to regroup and move forward after their disappointing losses.

stories.headline: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Hits 17-Ball 52, Forces Ex-India Star To Take U-Turn The 15-year-old silenced his critics in emphatic fashion, smashing 52 runs off just 17 balls, including four boundaries and five towering sixes.
stories.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, a 15-year-old batting prodigy, scored 52 runs off 17 balls, including four boundaries and five sixes, to power Rajasthan Royals to a commanding win over Chennai Super Kings in their IPL 2026 opening match. The left-hander's impressive knock helped the Royals chase down a modest target of 128 with nearly eight overs to spare, securing an 8-wicket win. Sooryavanshi's performance silenced his critics and showcased his immense talent, solidifying his position as a rising star in the Indian Premier League. As the youngest debutant in IPL history, he continues to make headlines with his exceptional batting skills, guiding the Royals to a comfortable victory in their first match of the season.

stories.headline: Tamil Nadu: TVK chief Vijay booked for violating election rules day after entering polls
stories.summary: Tamilaga Vetri Kazhagam (TVK) chief Vijay has been booked for allegedly violating election rules during his first campaign rally in Perambur, Chennai. The complaint filed against him stated that the gathering, which included nearly 5,000 party workers and 30 sound systems, obstructed a public pathway and blocked the movement of an ambulance, causing public inconvenience. The rally, held ahead of the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections on April 23, exceeded crowd limits and used unauthorized loudspeakers. The Peravallur police have registered a case under five sections, including public nuisance, unlawful assembly, and obstruction of public ways, naming Vijay and 5,000 party workers. The complaint alleged that the rally took place earlier than scheduled, at 3:40 pm, causing disturbance to the public and traffic. Legal action has been requested against those responsible for the violations.

stories.headline: Judiciary has failed to live up to people's expectations: Former Supreme Court Justice AS Oka
stories.summary: Former Supreme Court Justice AS Oka stated that the Indian judiciary has failed to meet the expectations of citizens as outlined in the Constitution. He cited issues such as delays in appointments and difficulties in obtaining bail as examples of the judiciary's shortcomings. Justice Oka made these comments during a lecture, emphasizing the importance of traditional litigation practices. He expressed concerns that the judiciary has not been able to deliver justice to common citizens, highlighting the need for improvement. The former justice's remarks come as a critique of the current state of the Indian judiciary, sparking discussion on the need for reform and increased efficiency in the judicial system to better serve the people. His comments have been widely reported and are being closely followed by legal experts and the public.

stories.headline: Centre Proposes To Extend IT Rules To Users Posting News Content On Social Media
stories.summary: The Indian government proposes to amend the IT Rules 2021, expanding regulatory oversight of online content. The changes aim to increase compliance for digital intermediaries and bring independent news creators on platforms like YouTube under a formal framework. The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting would gain powers for content removal and editorial changes. The proposed amendments would cover content hosted, displayed, or shared by non-publisher users on intermediary platforms, including social media. This move would bring news and current affairs shared by ordinary users under regulatory oversight, targeting user-generated content on platforms like Facebook, Google, and Instagram. The proposed changes aim to make social media platforms follow government directions more closely, monitoring and regulating user-generated news and current affairs content. The Ministry of Electronics and IT has proposed these sweeping changes to the digital media oversight framework.

stories.headline: Raja Raghuvanshi's family welcomes baby boy family believes he's been reborn
stories.summary: Raja Raghuvanshi's family has welcomed a baby boy, naming him "Raja", amidst the belief that he has been reborn into the family. This development comes months after Raja Raghuvanshi, an Indore businessman, was allegedly killed by his wife during their honeymoon in Meghalaya, near the Weisawdong Falls, on May 23, 2025. The family's emotional response to the newborn's arrival is rooted in their conviction that the baby is the reincarnation of Raja Raghuvanshi. The baby boy was born on Sunday, bringing a mix of emotions to the family as they cope with the loss of Raja while embracing the new addition to their family. The incident that led to Raja's death is still a point of reference, with his wife being implicated in the alleged murder.

stories.headline: US must let IAEA do its job in Iran
stories.summary: The US has sent a proposal to Iran for a nuclear deal, confirmed by the White House. Iran's foreign minister hinted that an agreement with the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) might be negotiable, allowing for the retrieval of Iran's highly enriched uranium (HEU) stockpile under IAEA supervision. Iran insists its nuclear program is peaceful, despite the IAEA report being described as "politically motivated" with "baseless accusations" by Iranian state media. Iranian politicians are pushing to exit the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT) amid increased US and Israeli attacks on civilian nuclear sites and other targets. The US must allow the IAEA to do its job in Iran, and the possibility of an IAEA agreement should be fully explored, with Iran seeking concessions including recognition of its international rights.

stories.headline: Modest dip in US futures on geopolitics, tech pressure despite Powell’s inflation assurance
stories.summary: US futures experienced a modest dip due to geopolitical tensions and tech sector pressure, despite assurances from Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell on inflation. Powell stated the Fed's commitment to bringing inflation back to its 2% target and signaled no rush in making policy changes amid global uncertainties. The dip in US futures occurred amid a surge in oil prices due to the Iran war, which has impacted the stock market. Powell's comments came during a moderated discussion, and another Fed official, John Williams, is scheduled to speak on the economy later. The situation is being closely monitored, with investors keeping a close eye on Treasury yields, interest rates, and oil prices, which are all factors influencing inflation and the overall economy. The Fed's stance is crucial in navigating the current economic landscape.

stories.headline: The shift to renewables is deeply tied to geopolitics, with supply chains increasingly dominated by China
stories.summary: The shift to renewables is closely tied to geopolitics, with China dominating supply chains. Renewable energy is converted to potential energy for storage in devices like electric batteries, which is later converted to electricity for the power grid. China's growing influence in the energy sector is set against a backdrop of global tensions, including its relations with Taiwan and the US. Recent disruptions, such as the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and strikes on Qatar's L.N.G. export complex, have removed 28 million tons of supply from the market, testing energy supply chains. Despite these challenges, China's export machine has become more diversified and resilient, adapting to war, sanctions, and political fragmentation. This has significant implications for the global energy landscape and the future of renewable energy. China's dominance in supply chains is likely to continue shaping the industry.

stories.headline: Investors deploy cash as volatility drives declines
stories.summary: Investors are deploying cash as market volatility drives declines. According to Devina Mehra of First Global, a 50-plus year study shows that equity markets consistently recover from geopolitical shocks. She advises long-term investors to deploy cash during market dips, as non-geopolitical events pose greater risks. Mehra remains optimistic about India's corporate earnings and highlights Iran's strategic approach to oil. When cash levels decline within equity schemes, it implies managers are finding deployable opportunities or responding to steady inflows. Conversely, rising aggregate cash may point to treasury management or preparation for volatility. Experts Russ Koesterich and Rick Rieder discuss how investors can prepare their portfolios for fading growth and rising volatility, emphasizing the need for a dynamic approach to navigate market shifts. Investors are taking a proactive approach to manage their portfolios amidst current geopolitical uncertainty.

stories.headline: Role Reversal: Tamil Nadu's Triumphs, Trends & Turmoils If Tamil Nadu politics before the nineties often resembled a game of snakes and ladders played under Delhi’s shadow, the decade after 1991 altered the board i..
stories.summary: Tamil Nadu's politics underwent a significant transformation after 1991, altering the state's dynamics and relationship with Delhi. The Dravidian Movement played a crucial role in shaping the state's identity and influence on India's democratic framework. The movement's ideals of social justice, linguistic pride, and welfare-oriented governance inspired major social reforms, including the abolition of hand-rickshaws. These reforms helped establish Tamil Nadu as a leader in social progress. The state's political legacy, rooted in the Dravidian Movement, has had a profound impact on its governance and identity. With a history of progressive policies and robust regional leadership, Tamil Nadu offers valuable lessons for governance and has reshaped politics, caste, and culture within India's democratic framework. The state's unique blend of ethnic identity and social justice has contributed to its distinct political landscape.

stories.headline: India at the end of Naxalism: How a violent movement was born, sustained and finally crushed — tracing the shift from political ambiguity to a decisive national security doctrine As India becomes free from the menace of Maoist-Leftist terrorism, here is a look back at the birth, life and death of Naxalism in India.
stories.summary: India is nearing the end of Naxalism, a violent Maoist-Leftist movement that originated in 1967 in Naxalbari. The movement, also known as Left Wing Extremism (LWE), has been a significant internal security challenge, affecting over 120 districts, particularly in remote and underdeveloped tribal areas. Fueled by socio-economic inequalities and Maoist ideology, LWE aimed to undermine the Indian state through armed rebellion. The government has implemented a multi-faceted approach, including strong governance, equitable development, and targeted counterinsurgency operations, leading to a sharp decrease in Naxal violence. With a decisive national security doctrine in place, India is expected to become Naxal-free by March 2026, marking the end of a 5-decade-long internal armed conflict that has spanned multiple governments and regions. Key data and achievements indicate significant progress in resolving the long-standing extremism.

stories.headline: (Note: Since there are only two headlines provided, I couldn't select four. The above response includes the two given headlines. If more headlines were provided, I would be happy to assist further.)
stories.summary: Timothy Evans, Cook County's chief judge for 24 years, faced two challengers in the September 10 election. Meanwhile, in the financial sector, the integration of First Republic Bank after its purchase has alleviated its failure as a negative issue for the US economy. In other news, a lawsuit filed by former HR Director Babette Friedman, alleging civil rights violations and a hostile work environment, was dismissed. The lawsuit sought $2 million in damages. Additionally, investment opportunities in non-US equities, deep value stocks, and liquid alternatives are currently offering reasonable or better returns, regardless of the impact of AI on the market. These developments highlight various aspects of the economy, law, and finance, showcasing the complexities of the current landscape.

stories.headline: GIFT Nifty jumps 240 points on Trump's reported plans to end Iran war
stories.summary: GIFT Nifty jumped 240 points to trade at 22,672, following reports of US President Donald Trump's plans to end the Iran war. This surge occurred despite Indian stock markets being closed for Shri Mahavir Jayanti. Earlier, GIFT Nifty had risen 90 points on Trump's reported plan to end Iran strikes, and also surged 3 percent after he delayed Iran strikes. Brent oil futures dropped over 14 percent to $96 a barrel. GIFT Nifty futures also increased, rising 78.5 points to 23,148.50, indicating a 0.34% upside. The rebound in Indian equities is attributed to the improvement in global risk sentiment and the crash in oil prices. The market's positive reaction suggests a potential uptrend in Indian stocks when trading resumes.

stories.headline: Sensex, Nifty 50 close FY26 on a sour note
stories.summary: India's stock market ended FY26 on a weak note, with the Sensex plummeting 1,635 points to 71,947 and the Nifty 50 losing 488 points to 22,331. This marked the market's weakest fiscal performance since FY20. The Nifty 50 declined 1,009.70 points, or 4.33%, in FY26, while the Sensex fell 4,935.03 points, or 6.42%. The final session wiped out nearly ₹10 lakh crore from the market capitalization of BSE-listed firms, which fell to ₹412.43 lakh crore. The close below the 22,500 support zone signals a continuation of the broader downtrend. The market's poor performance was characterized by sustained selling pressure throughout the day, with defence and financials being the only sectors to shine, while IT and consumption lagged behind. The market was closed on March 31 due to Shri Mahavir Jayanti.

stories.headline: Stock Market Holiday Today: Are Sensex, Nifty Closed On Mahavir Jayanti? Check BSE, NSE Status On March 31
stories.summary: The Indian stock market is closed today, March 31, in observance of Mahavir Jayanti. Both the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and National Stock Exchange (NSE) are shut for trading, as per their official holiday list. The closure affects equity, equity derivatives, SLB segments, bonds, commodities, and rupee trading, among others. This is the first of two market holidays this week, with the next one scheduled for Good Friday. Trading will resume on Friday. The stock market will also be closed on April 14 for Dr. Baba Saheb Ambedkar Jayanti. The BSE and NSE will remain closed today to mark the festival, and normal trading activities will resume after the holiday. This closure is as per the scheduled holiday list for 2026, announced by the exchanges earlier.

stories.headline: Sensex, Nifty to open in green tomorrow? GIFT Nifty rises over 100 points on Trump’s reported plans to end Iran war
stories.summary: Indian stock markets are expected to open in the green tomorrow after a positive run on Tuesday. The GIFT Nifty rose over 100 points following US President Donald Trump's reported plans to end the Iran war. Trump paused strikes on Iranian energy sites for 5 days, boosting investor sentiment. The Nifty50 opened at 23,064.40, gaining 152 points or 0.66%, while the BSE Sensex rose by 581.67 points or 0.79% to open at 74,650.12. The Trump government has offered a 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,144, indicating a likely opening below the previous close of 23,306.45 on the Nifty50. The positive sentiment is expected to continue, with markets poised for a rebound, potentially leading to a gap-up opening tomorrow.

stories.headline: BNM keeps Malaysia’s 2026 growth momentum steady, projecting 4-5pc even in face of war
stories.summary: Malaysia's economy is projected to grow between 4% and 5% in 2026, according to Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM). This forecast remains steady despite the ongoing war in the Middle East, which has led to increased crude oil prices and energy crises in some countries. The central bank attributes the sustained growth momentum to domestic demand, steady tourism, and strong exports, particularly in the technology sector. Malaysia's status as a net energy exporter is also expected to help buffer the economy from external shocks. In 2025, the country's gross domestic product rose 5.2%, driven by record-high investments. BNM's forecast suggests that Malaysia's economy will continue to expand, driven by its strong fundamentals and resilience to global uncertainties. The bank's projection indicates a positive outlook for the country's economic growth in 2026.

stories.headline: Elderly people left 'starving' in bed at Liverpool care home
stories.summary: Elderly residents at Christopher Grange Residential Care in Liverpool were left "starving" in bed, according to a shocking report. Inspectors found severe neglect at the care home, with residents experiencing pain after calling for help and having their belongings go missing. Incontinence pads were also found scattered around the garden. The care home, which provides personal care to older people, has been slammed by inspectors, prompting action to be taken against it. Similarly, another Liverpool care home, Stonedale Lodge in Croxteth, was placed in special measures after an inspection found residents without clothing or toiletries. Both care homes have been criticized for their poor care and neglect of residents, highlighting concerns about the welfare and safety of elderly people in their care. Inspectors have taken action to address these issues and ensure residents receive proper care.

stories.headline: Consumers keen on direct purchasing via AI platforms, Kantar claims
stories.summary: Kantar's consumer research reveals that one-third of UK respondents would prefer to buy directly through ChatGPT and other generative AI platforms rather than visiting a retailer's website. Additionally, one in four consumers now ask AI for product recommendations. The study found that 41% of people are "all in" on AI, being excited by its possibilities and fully trusting and embracing it. This trend indicates a growing influence of AI in consumer purchasing decisions. The data suggests that consumers are becoming increasingly comfortable with using AI platforms for direct purchases, potentially changing the way retailers interact with customers and sell their products. Kantar's research highlights the shifting consumer behavior and the importance of AI in the retail industry.

stories.headline: Over 47,000 cabbies adopt Octopus, card readers ahead of e-payment deadline
stories.summary: More than 47,000 taxi drivers in Hong Kong have adopted the commercial version of the Octopus app or equipped their cabs with mobile card readers. This move comes ahead of a deadline for the implementation of electronic payments in taxis. The adoption of Octopus, a popular payment method in Hong Kong, or mobile card readers will enable passengers to pay their fares electronically. The transition to electronic payments is expected to enhance convenience and efficiency for both taxi drivers and passengers. With over 47,000 cabbies already on board, the majority of the city's taxi fleet is now prepared for the upcoming deadline, ensuring a smooth transition to electronic payments. The widespread adoption of electronic payment methods is set to modernize Hong Kong's taxi industry.

stories.headline: Trump helped build the Middle East’s AI ambitions, Could his war break them?
stories.summary: President Donald Trump's visit to the Middle East last spring led to deals that boosted the region's artificial intelligence ambitions. The agreements aimed to vault the Gulf into the global AI race. However, a subsequent war sparked by Trump has put these ambitions at risk. The conflict has made the region's AI infrastructure a target, threatening the progress made. The Middle East's AI empire, valued at $500 billion, relies on cloud computing, which has a physical presence and is vulnerable to attacks. The war has raised concerns about the stability and security of the region's AI industry, potentially undermining the gains made during Trump's visit. The situation highlights the fragility of the Middle East's AI ambitions and the need for a stable environment to support their growth.

stories.headline: Google Unveils New AI Model That Can Learn Without Human Supervision
stories.summary: Google has unveiled a new AI model that can learn without human supervision, marking a significant breakthrough in artificial intelligence research. The model, developed by Google researchers, uses a novel training method that enables AI systems to update and learn on their own. This approach allows large language models to learn more like humans, without relying on human-labeled data. The development is part of Google's ongoing efforts to advance AI capabilities, including its recent introduction of Natively Adaptive Interfaces and the Gemini 3.1 model. The new AI model has the potential to revolutionize various applications, including education, with an AI-powered school set to open in Chicago this fall. The technology is expected to have a significant impact on how AI systems are trained and used in the future.

stories.headline: Microsoft Invests $1 Billion in OpenAI to Develop Exclusive AI Technologies
stories.summary: Microsoft is investing over $1 billion in Thailand from 2026 to 2028 to develop cloud and artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure. The investment will focus on building data centers in partnership with local companies. This move aims to meet the growing demand for AI technologies in the region. The investment will cover cloud and AI infrastructure, including ongoing operations, to support the development of exclusive AI technologies. Microsoft's commitment to Thailand's digital transformation is expected to enhance the country's technological capabilities and support innovation. The investment is part of Microsoft's broader strategy to expand its presence in the region and capitalize on the increasing demand for cloud and AI solutions. This significant investment underscores Microsoft's confidence in Thailand's potential for digital growth and development.

stories.headline: AI System Beats Human World Champion in Strategic Game of No-Limit Poker
stories.summary: An artificial intelligence system has beaten a human world champion in a strategic game of no-limit poker. The AI, which competed against human players during the day, perfected its strategy overnight by analyzing its gameplay. This is not the first time an AI has beaten humans in poker, as a bot called Pluribus was successful in 2019, playing no-limit Texas hold'em and beating humans in a complex multiplayer competition. Another AI system, DeepStack, also defeated professional poker players in 2016. The AI's ability to play for hours without fatigue and endure game variances without emotional influence gives it an edge over human players. The recent tournament win saw the AI emerge victorious, although it did not receive prize money as per the tournament rules. The AI's developers continue to refine its strategy, making it a formidable opponent in the game of poker.
